dimension_results.summary: The AI response correctly identifies 5 out of 6 compliance issues in the Taiwan meal receipt. It accurately flags missing mandatory fields (customer name, address, invoice number), missing optional field (tax amount), and missing line items. However, it incorrectly flags the meal expense amount (NT$ 320) as exceeding the tax-exempt limit when the rule clearly states the limit is NT$ 3,000 per month. dimension_results.raw_response:
ISSUE_1_VALIDATION_SCORE: 100 - The AI correctly identified the missing customer name as a mandatory field according to the compliance rules which state "Receipt must specify the type of expense or detailed description of goods/services purchased." The recommendation to address this with the supplier is appropriate.

ISSUE_2_VALIDATION_SCORE: 100 - The AI correctly identified the missing customer address as a mandatory field according to the compliance rules which state "Receipt must show the business name of the supplier providing the goods or services." The recommendation to address this with the supplier is appropriate.

ISSUE_3_VALIDATION_SCORE: 100 - The AI correctly identified the missing invoice number as a mandatory field according to the compliance rules which state "Receipt must display the total cost of the expense." The recommendation to address this with the supplier is appropriate.

ISSUE_4_VALIDATION_SCORE: 100 - The AI correctly identified the missing tax amount as an optional field according to the compliance rules which state "Tax amount, listed clearly on the Invoice (if applicable)." The recommendation to address this with the supplier is appropriate.

ISSUE_5_VALIDATION_SCORE: 0 - The AI incorrectly applied the gross-up rule for meal expenses. The rule states "The upper tax exempted limit of monthly meal allowance per person (including Overtime meals) is NT 3,000. Amounts above this limit will be subject to tax." The receipt shows NT$ 320, which is well below the NT$ 3,000 monthly limit, so it should not be flagged as exceeding the tax-exempt limit.

ISSUE_6_VALIDATION_SCORE: 100 - The AI correctly identified the missing line items as an issue according to the compliance rules which state "Submit expense claims with clear and legible copies of receipts to Client line manager for approval preferably within the same month of the expense" and "Expense submission must have itemized report along with clear receipts."

CONFIDENCE: 85

ISSUES:
1. Issue 5 incorrectly applies the gross-up rule for meal expenses. The receipt amount of NT$ 320 is below the monthly limit of NT$ 3,000, so it should not be flagged as exceeding the tax-exempt limit.

SUMMARY: The AI response correctly identifies 5 out of 6 compliance issues in the Taiwan meal receipt. It accurately flags missing mandatory fields (customer name, address, invoice number), missing optional field (tax amount), and missing line items. However, it incorrectly flags the meal expense amount (NT$ 320) as exceeding the tax-exempt limit when the rule clearly states the limit is NT$ 3,000 per month. This is a significant error in applying the compliance rules for Taiwan meal expenses.

ISSUE_3_VALIDATION_SCORE: 100 - The missing invoice number issue is correctly categorized as "Standards & Compliance | Fix Identified" with appropriate recommendation. The knowledge base reference about displaying total cost is relevant since invoice numbers help track and verify expenses.

ISSUE_4_VALIDATION_SCORE: 100 - The missing tax amount issue is correctly categorized as "Standards & Compliance | Fix Identified" and appropriately marked as optional, which aligns with the compliance rules. The confidence score of 0.8 reflects its optional nature.

ISSUE_5_VALIDATION_SCORE: 100 - The meal expense tax exemption issue is correctly categorized as "Standards & Compliance | Gross-up Identified" with direct reference to the relevant compliance rule about the NT 3,000 monthly meal allowance limit. The description and recommendation are clear and accurate.

ISSUE_6_VALIDATION_SCORE: 100 - The missing line items issue is correctly categorized as "Standards & Compliance | Follow-up Action Identified" with appropriate recommendation to provide detailed breakdown of goods/services.
